name: Add a feature flag
description: Introduce a new env-driven feature flag, document it, default it to off, and gate the new behaviour behind it.
architectHints: Default the flag to off so a deploy is a no-op until an operator opts in.
Use the existing REDDWARF_*_ENABLED naming pattern, e.g. REDDWARF_FOO_ENABLED.
Document the new flag in both .env.example (with a comment explaining what it does) and docs/reference/CONFIG.md (in the right section).
If the flag gates a new code path, include at least one test that exercises both states.
validatorRules: .env.example must contain the new variable with an explanatory comment.
docs/reference/CONFIG.md must document the new variable in the appropriate section.
Default must be the off / safer state.
reviewerRubric: Is the flag boundary in the right place — do callers branch on the flag, or does the flag flow into a single check site?
What's the rollback story? Flipping the flag back to off should leave no orphaned state.
